event: One Knoxville SC defeated Fort Wayne FC 2-1 at Covenant Health Park, reclaiming first place in USL League One and extending their unbeaten streak to eight matches.
Fort Wayne FC lost 1-2 to One Knoxville SC; player Javier Armas received a red card off the opening kickoff, leaving the team a man down from the second minute.
Fort Wayne FC announced a multiyear naming rights partnership with Ruoff Mortgage, establishing Ruoff Mortgage Stadium as the official name of the club's new soccer-specific venue.
Miami FC defeated Indy Eleven 2-0 on the road per publicly circulated results summary.
Indy Eleven lost 0-2 at home to Miami FC per publicly circulated results summary.
New Mexico United defeated Lexington SC 4-1 away per publicly circulated results summary.
Lexington SC lost 1-4 at home to New Mexico United per publicly circulated results summary.
Sporting JAX defeated defending league champions Pittsburgh Riverhounds 2-1 per publicly circulated results summary.
Pittsburgh Riverhounds, defending USL Championship league champions, lost 1-2 to expansion side Sporting JAX per publicly circulated results summary.

Lionsbridge FC host Asheville City SC in the USL League Two Eastern Conference Quarterfinal at TowneBank Stadium; Lionsbridge entered as Chesapeake Division champions with 34 points and +32 goal differential.
Vermont Green FC host Ironbound SC in the USL League Two Eastern Conference Quarterfinal at Virtue Field, Burlington, as reigning league playoff champions.
Northern Indiana FC clinched a USL League Two playoff berth on the final day of the regular season in just its second year in the league, turning around a 3-4 start with five consecutive victories.

LVU Rush qualified for the 2026 USL League Two playoffs with 10 wins in their first year under new coaches Eric Lambinus and Tyler Nolan, completing one of the league's biggest year-over-year turnarounds.
USL League Two published a club profile feature on Minneapolis City SC highlighting their Minnesota-first community club model built with longevity in mind.

North Carolina FC suspended operations following the 2025 season and formally applied to join USL Premier (Division One) targeting 2028 entry, contingent on securing a minimum 15,000-seat purpose-built stadium in Raleigh.
A two-time USL Championship MVP joined Texoma FC as assistant coach in USL League Two, adding senior professional experience to a club that self-relegated from League One for 2026.
